title: Toronto 7-Day City Break
metaTitle: Toronto 7-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 7-day trip to Toronto, Canada: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including CN Tower and Royal Ontario Museum (ROM). Prices from $1053 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 7 days discovering Toronto, Canada — Humid continental climate with cold, snowy winters (highs near or below 0°C, Dec-Feb) and warm, humid summers (highs around 27°C, Jun-Aug). May to September brings warm days for exploring waterfront and patios; September-October adds fall foliage with lighter crowds than summer. This itinerary blends CN Tower, Royal Ontario Museum (ROM), Distillery District with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
6 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including CN Tower, Royal Ontario Museum (ROM), Distillery District
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Toronto
itinerary.description: Land at Toronto Pearson International Airport (YYZ) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to CN Tower — Iconic 553m tower with glass floor and EdgeWalk experience. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Toronto: Royal Ontario Museum (ROM) & Ripley's Aquarium of Canada
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Royal Ontario Museum (ROM) — Canada's largest natural history and world culture museum. In the afternoon, continue to Ripley's Aquarium of Canada — Large downtown aquarium beside the CN Tower. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Toronto: Casa Loma & Art Gallery of Ontario (AGO)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Casa Loma — Gothic Revival castle with gardens and secret tunnels. In the afternoon, continue to Art Gallery of Ontario (AGO) — Major art museum with a Frank Gehry-designed facade. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Niagara Falls
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Niagara Falls, a popular side trip from Toronto. Return to Toronto in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Cloud Gardens Conservatory — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Toronto: Toronto Zoo & Hockey Hall of Fame
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Toronto Zoo — One of the largest zoos in the world by area. In the afternoon, continue to Hockey Hall of Fame — Shrine to hockey history including the original Stanley Cup. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Ontario Science Centre or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to YYZ for your departure flight.

travelTips: Buy CN Tower and Ripley's Aquarium combo tickets online in advance to skip long queues.
The UP Express airport train is faster and cheaper than a taxi from Pearson to downtown.
Book Niagara Falls day tours or car rentals ahead in summer — it's a very popular day trip.
TIFF (September) and Caribana (July/August) both cause hotel prices to spike — book early.
St. Lawrence Market is closed on Sundays and Mondays — plan your visit around that.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
A heavy winter coat, boots and layers if visiting December-February — cold is serious
Light layers for humid summer days that can turn cool at night
Comfortable shoes for extensive subway/streetcar walking
An umbrella for year-round rain showers
thingsToDo: St. Lawrence Market exterior and stalls
thingsToAvoid: Underestimating winter cold — wind chill can make it feel far colder than the forecast high suggests.
Driving downtown during rush hour — Toronto traffic and parking costs are notoriously bad.
Assuming US dollars are accepted everywhere — carry or withdraw Canadian dollars for smaller vendors.
Skipping travel insurance — Canadian healthcare is not free for foreign visitors and costs add up quickly.
Jaywalking near streetcar tracks — streetcars can't swerve and take longer to stop than buses.
topRestaurants: St. Lawrence Market vendors — Peameal Bacon Sandwiches ($)
Khao San Road — Thai Street Food ($$)
instagramSpots: Toronto sign at Nathan Phillips Square
hiddenGems: Elgin and Winter Garden Theatre Centre
adventureActivities: EdgeWalk hands-free walk around the CN Tower rim
Cycling the Martin Goodman waterfront trail
Rock climbing at a Distillery District gym
luxuryExperiences: Private Niagara wine country day tour
Suite stay at the Ritz-Carlton Toronto
VIP box at a Maple Leafs or Raptors game
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset dinner cruise on Lake Ontario
Romantic stroll through the Distillery District at dusk
Couples spa day at a downtown luxury hotel
budgetExperiences: Free ferry-adjacent wander of the Toronto Islands
Browse Kensington Market and Graffiti Alley
Cherry blossoms in High Park (spring, free)
localEtiquette: Canadians are famously polite — saying "sorry" even when it isn't your fault is normal, and reciprocating is appreciated.
Tipping 15-20% at restaurants and bars is standard and expected.
Queueing (lining up) is taken seriously — cutting a line is a real social faux pas.
Toronto is one of the world's most multicultural cities; neighborhoods reflect distinct communities that deserve respectful visiting.
Escalator etiquette: stand right, walk left, especially in the subway.
localTransportGuide: The TTC subway, streetcars and buses cover the city well — tap a PRESTO card or contactless bank card for one fare across the system. The UP Express train runs directly from Pearson Airport to Union Station downtown in about 25 minutes, far faster than a taxi in traffic.
bestTimeToVisit: May to September brings warm days for exploring waterfront and patios; September-October adds fall foliage with lighter crowds than summer.
weather: Humid continental climate with cold, snowy winters (highs near or below 0°C, Dec-Feb) and warm, humid summers (highs around 27°C, Jun-Aug).
visaInformation: Visa-exempt eTA (Electronic Travel Authorization) for UK, EU, Australian and other eligible passport holders arriving by air for short stays. Indian nationals and most other nationalities require a Canadian visitor visa (TRV) arranged in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~13.8h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Toronto?
faqs.answer: Visa-exempt eTA (Electronic Travel Authorization) for UK, EU, Australian and other eligible passport holders arriving by air for short stays. Indian nationals and most other nationalities require a Canadian visitor visa (TRV) arranged in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Toronto?
faqs.answer: May to September brings warm days for exploring waterfront and patios; September-October adds fall foliage with lighter crowds than summer. Humid continental climate with cold, snowy winters (highs near or below 0°C, Dec-Feb) and warm, humid summers (highs around 27°C, Jun-Aug).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Toronto safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Toronto is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Toronto?
faqs.answer: The local currency is CAD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Emma Brown
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Our trip to Toronto was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to St. Lawrence Market food tour was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Marco Smith
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Chinatown and Little Italy and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Nadia Smith
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Toronto is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Kensington Market. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: James Nguyen
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: Our trip to Toronto was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Toronto Islands was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Yuki Nguyen
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Art Gallery of Ontario (AGO) alone was worth the trip, but combined with Art Gallery of Ontario (AGO) this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.
